2026-04-23T22:16:24.0974033Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T22:16:24.0974476Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T22:16:24.0974831Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T22:16:24.0975131Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T22:16:24.0975380Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T22:16:24.0975574Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T22:16:24.0975706Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-04-23T22:19:18.7476976Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T22:19:09.983612128Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Storage` throws `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` when deserializing storage accounts with the Smart Tier access tier, because `StorageAccountAccessTier.Smart` is missing from the SDK's enum/extension.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Details\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Storage 1.6.2`\n**Affected API:** `StorageAccountAccessTierExtensions.ToStorageAccountAccessTier`, `StorageAccountProperties.DeserializeStorageAccountProperties`\n**Scenarios:**\n- Calling `GetStorageAccountAsync(name)` on a storage account that was converted to Smart Tier via the Azure portal throws an unhandled `ArgumentOutOfRangeException`\n- The `Smart` access tier value returned by the Azure service is not recognized by the SDK\n\n**Root ask:** Add `StorageAccountAccessTier.Smart` to the SDK so accounts using Smart Tier can be retrieved without exceptions.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe exception is thrown in `StorageAccountAccessTierExtensions.ToStorageAccountAccessTier(string value)` when the REST response contains `\"Smart\"` as the `accessTier` property. The SDK's extensible enum does not include this value, causing deserialization to fail.\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1. Confirm the Azure Storage REST API now surfaces `\"Smart\"` as a valid `accessTier` value and identify the API version where it was introduced\n2. Add `StorageAccountAccessTier.Smart` to `StorageAccountAccessTier` (or the equivalent extensible enum) and ensure `ToStorageAccountAccessTier` handles it\n3. Verify the TypeSpec/Swagger spec is updated to include the `Smart` tier value so future regeneration does not regress this fix\n\n**Note:** This appears to be a duplicate of open issue #55062, which reports the same `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` for `StorageAccountAccessTier.Smart` on `Azure.ResourceManager.Storage 1.4.2` / 1.6.0. The root cause and fix are identical.\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🏷️ Label Confidence\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Category:** `Mgmt` — Package is `Azure.ResourceManager.Storage`, which is a management-plane SDK\n- **Service:** `Storage` — Directly about Azure Storage account management\n- **Confidence:** High — package name and service are unambiguous; no other category or service labels are plausible\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e👥 Owner Routing\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 888) — matched because no more-specific `%Storage %Mgmt` combo entry exists; bottom-to-top scan reached the `%Mgmt` catch-all first\n- **AzureSdkOwners:** @ArthurMa1978\n- **ServiceOwners:** none listed for this entry\n- **Routing action:** Assigned `@ArthurMa1978`; added `needs-team-attention`\n- **Scan notes:** All `%\u003cService\u003e %Mgmt` entries after line 888 (e.g., `%SQL %Mgmt`, `%Oracle %Mgmt`, `%NetApp Files %Mgmt`) were scanned first but did not include a `%Storage %Mgmt` entry.
